title: Basic
summary: Models a conformal structure as a metric bundle with positive rescaling, together with scales and the comparison of representatives.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Models a conformal structure as a metric bundle with positive rescaling, together with scales and the comparison of representatives.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Conformal/Basic.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: the pos real is defined to be Units NNReal.

A bare structure encoding the existence of concrete metrics on M. PointMetric x is the type of actual metrics at the point x. A global metric field is then a section ∀ x, PointMetric x. Its mathematical data are: Point Metric: M maps to Type v.
A global metric field is simply a section of pointwise metrics. In explicit terms, its defining expression is for every x : M, Met.Point Metric x.

An intrinsic conformal structure on M. For each x : M, the fiber MetricFiber x should be thought of as the ray of metrics at x inside the conformal class. The action of PosReal is the dilation g ↦ r^2 g in the geometric picture; here we suppress the square and use a single positive parameter abstractly. Its mathematical data are: Metric Fiber: M maps to Type v; to Fiber: for every x : M, Point Metric x maps to Metric Fiber x; dilate: for every x : M, Pos Real maps to Metric Fiber x maps to Metric Fiber x; dilate one: for every x : M g : Metric Fiber x, dilate 1 g equals g; dilate mul: for every x : M a b : Pos Real g : Metric Fiber x, dilate a times b g equals dilate a dilate b g; dilate exists: for every x : M g h : Metric Fiber x, there exists r : Pos Real, dilate r g equals h; dilate injective: for every x : M g : Metric Fiber x r s : Pos Real, dilate r g equals dilate s g maps to r equals s; dilate Metric: for every x : M, Pos Real maps to Point Metric x maps to Point Metric x; dilate Metric one: for every x : M g : Point Metric x, dilate Metric 1 g equals g; dilate Metric mul: for every x : M a b : Pos Real g : Point Metric x, dilate Metric a times b g equals dilate Metric a dilate Metric b g; dilate Metric injective: for every x : M a : Pos Real, Function.Injective dilate Metric x : equals x a; to Fiber dilate Metric: for every x : M a : Pos Real g : Point Metric x, to Fiber dilate Metric a g equals dilate a to Fiber g.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{MetricFiber}&\colon M \longrightarrow \mathrm{Type} v\\\operatorname{toFiber}&\colon \forall {x \colon M}, \operatorname{PointMetric} x \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricFiber} x\\\operatorname{dilate}&\colon \forall {x \colon M}, \operatorname{PosReal} \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricFiber} x \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricFiber} x\\\operatorname{dilate\_one}&\colon \forall {x \colon M} (g \colon \operatorname{MetricFiber} x), \operatorname{dilate} 1 g = g\\\operatorname{dilate\_mul}&\colon \forall {x \colon M} (a b \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (g \colon \operatorname{MetricFiber} x), \operatorname{dilate} (a \cdot b) g = \operatorname{dilate} a (\operatorname{dilate} b g)\\\operatorname{dilate\_exists}&\colon \forall {x \colon M} (g h \colon \operatorname{MetricFiber} x), \exists r \colon \operatorname{PosReal}, \operatorname{dilate} r g = h\\\operatorname{dilate\_injective}&\colon \forall {x \colon M} {g \colon \operatorname{MetricFiber} x} {r s \colon \operatorname{PosReal}}, \operatorname{dilate} r g = \operatorname{dilate} s g \longrightarrow r = s\\\operatorname{dilateMetric}&\colon \forall {x \colon M}, \operatorname{PosReal} \longrightarrow \operatorname{PointMetric} x \longrightarrow \operatorname{PointMetric} x\\\operatorname{dilateMetric\_one}&\colon \forall {x \colon M} (g \colon \operatorname{PointMetric} x), \operatorname{dilateMetric} 1 g = g\\\operatorname{dilateMetric\_mul}&\colon \forall {x \colon M} (a b \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (g \colon \operatorname{PointMetric} x), \operatorname{dilateMetric} (a \cdot b) g = \operatorname{dilateMetric} a (\operatorname{dilateMetric} b g)\\\operatorname{dilateMetric\_injective}&\colon \forall {x \colon M} (a \colon \operatorname{PosReal}), \operatorname{Function}.\operatorname{Injective} (\operatorname{dilateMetric} (x = x) a)\\\operatorname{toFiber\_dilateMetric}&\colon \forall {x \colon M} (a \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (g \colon \operatorname{PointMetric} x), \operatorname{toFiber} (\operatorname{dilateMetric} a g) = \operatorname{dilate} a (\operatorname{toFiber} g)\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure ConformalStructure (M : Type u) extends MetricStructure M

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For x of type M, the pointwise metric is defined to be Conf.Point Metric x.
the metric field is defined to be for every x : M, Conf.Point Metric x.
The total space of the metric bundle associated to a conformal structure. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Σ x : M, Conf.Point Metric x.
the metric bundle is defined to be Σ x : M, Conf.Metric Fiber x.
Projection from the metric bundle to the base manifold. In explicit terms, for u of type metric bundle conf, its defining expression is u.1.
For u of type Point Metric Bundle Conf, the pointproj is defined to be u.1.
Dilation on the total space of the metric bundle. In explicit terms, for r of type pos real, its defining expression is | ⟨x, g⟩ maps to ⟨x, Conf.dilate r g⟩.
Dilation on the concrete metric bundle. In explicit terms, for a of type pos real, its defining expression is | ⟨x, g⟩ maps to ⟨x, Conf.dilate Metric a g⟩.

A scale is a choice of one point in each conformal ray, i.e. a representative metric in the conformal class. Its mathematical data are: totalmetric: Conf.Metric Field; metric: for every x : M, Conf.Metric Fiber x; compat: for every x : M, Conf.to Fiber totalmetric x equals metric x.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{totalmetric}&\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricField}\\\operatorname{metric}&\colon \forall x \colon M, \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricFiber} x\\\operatorname{compat}&\colon \forall x \colon M, \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{toFiber} (\operatorname{totalmetric} x) = \operatorname{metric} x\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The induced section of the metric bundle. In explicit terms, for m a type, conf of type conformal structure m, and s of type conformal structure.scale conf, its defining expression is the function sending x to ⟨x, s.metric x⟩.
correspondence.leanType: def Scale.section {M : Type u} {Conf : ConformalStructure M} (s : ConformalStructure.Scale Conf) : M → ConformalStructure.MetricBundle Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The positive dilation factor taking the chosen scale to a given point in the same fiber. In explicit terms, for s of type conformal structure.scale conf, x of type m, and g of type conf.metric fiber x, its defining expression is Classical.choose Conf.dilate exists s.metric x g.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def scaleFactor (s : ConformalStructure.Scale Conf) {x : M} (g : Conf.MetricFiber x) : PosReal

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The trivialization of the metric bundle induced by a scale: each point of the bundle is uniquely (r, x) with g = r • s(x).
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def trivialization (s : ConformalStructure.Scale Conf) : Conf.MetricBundle ≃ PosReal × M

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The fiber over a point is nonempty once a scale is chosen. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem fiber_nonempty (s : ConformalStructure.Scale Conf) (x : M) : Nonempty (Conf.MetricFiber x)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Any two scales differ by a unique positive function on M. In explicit terms, for s₁, s₂ of type conf.scale, its defining expression is the function sending x to Conf.scale Factor s₁ s₂.metric x.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def relativeFactor (s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale) : M → PosReal

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Reconstructing one scale from another and the relative positive function. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,s₁,s₂\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{Scale},\quad s₂.\operatorname{metric} = x \longmapsto \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{dilate} (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{relativeFactor} s₁ s₂ x) (s₁.\operatorname{metric} x)
correspondence.leanType: theorem scale_eq_dilate_relativeFactor (s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale) : s₂.metric = fun x => Conf.dilate (Conf.relativeFactor s₁ s₂ x) (s₁.metric x)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Projection from concrete metric bundle to ray bundle. In explicit terms, its defining expression is | ⟨x, g⟩ maps to ⟨x, Conf.to Fiber g⟩.

Concrete dilation of a metric field by a positive function. In explicit terms, for σ of type m maps to pos real, and g of type conf.metric field, its defining expression is the function sending x to Conf.dilate Metric σ x g x.
correspondence.leanType: def dilateMetricField (σ : M → PosReal) (g : Conf.MetricField) : Conf.MetricField

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Constant dilation of a metric field. In explicit terms, for a of type pos real, and g of type conf.metric field, its defining expression is the function sending x to Conf.dilate Metric a g x.

If you want a scale obtained from constant dilation of a concrete representative. In explicit terms, for s of type conf.scale, and a of type pos real, its defining expression is totalmetric : equals Conf.dilate Metric Field Const a s.totalmetric metric : equals the function sending x to Conf.dilate a s.metric x compat : equals by intro x calc Conf.to Fiber Conf.dilate Metric Field Const a s.totalmetric x equals Conf.dilate a Conf.to Fiber s.totalmetric x : equals by simp Conformal Structure.dilate Metric Field Const, Conf.to Fiber dilate Metric equals Conf.dilate a s.metric x : equals by rw s.compat x.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{Scale}.\operatorname{dilateTotalMetric}(s,a)=\operatorname{totalmetric} = \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{dilateMetricFieldConst} a s.\operatorname{totalmetric} \operatorname{metric} = x \longmapsto \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{dilate} a (s.\operatorname{metric} x) \operatorname{compat} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} x \operatorname{calc} \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{toFiber} (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{dilateMetricFieldConst} a s.\operatorname{totalmetric} x) = \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{dilate} a (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{toFiber} (s.\operatorname{totalmetric} x)) = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{simp} [\operatorname{ConformalStructure}.\operatorname{dilateMetricFieldConst}, \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{toFiber\_dilateMetric}] _ = \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{dilate} a (s.\operatorname{metric} x) = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{rw} [s.\operatorname{compat} x]

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
